body: ## Summary Implements all six code-level fixes (R1â€“R6) from the issue #92 case study so both the JS and Rust CLIs produce the expected Google Docs markdown through the browser and API capture paths. Ships alongside the case study under `docs/case-studies/issue-92/`. ### Fixes in this PR - **R1 Rust browser parity** â€” `rust/src/gdocs.rs`: treat `0x0a` inside tables as a cell separator, drop trailing empty cells/rows, render ordered lists with a per-(list,level) counter that resets deeper levels, and join same-list items with a single newline. - **R2 JS browser multi-column tables** â€” `js/src/gdocs.js`: same cell-separator handling as R1 so multi-column rows are no longer collapsed into one cell. - **R3 JS browser sequential ordered list numbering** â€” per-(list,level) counter so items render as `1. 2. 3.` instead of every item starting at `1.`. - **R4 JS browser list item spacing** â€” single-newline separator between same-list items (matches CommonMark tight lists); keeps `\n\n` between different lists. - **R5 JS archive image download** â€” new `js/src/gdocs-images.js` downloads `docs-images-rt/...` URLs and rewrites markdown/html to `images/` so archive-mode captures are self-contained. - **R6 Shared API-mode export HTML preprocessor** â€” new `js/src/gdocs-preprocess.js` + matching `preprocess_google_docs_export_html` in `rust/src/gdocs.rs`: - hoists `font-weight:bold|700+`, `font-style:italic`, `text-decoration:line-through` spans into `//` before Turndown/html2md run; - strips the empty `` anchors and `N. ` / `1.2. ` numbering spans Google Docs injects into headings; - unwraps `google.com/url?q=` redirect wrappers back to the original target; - normalizes ` ` / U+00A0 to regular spaces. ### Tests - JS: `js/tests/unit/gdocs.test.js` â€” 54 tests pass, including the new R5 (2 tests) and R6 (7 tests) cases. - Rust: `rust/tests/integration/gdocs.rs` â€” 41 tests pass, including R2, R3, R4 and 7 R6 cases. - Both workspaces pass `npm run lint`, `npm run format:check`, `cargo fmt --check`, and `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`. ### Notes - Extracted `preprocessGoogleDocsExportHtml` and `localizeGoogleDocsModelImages` into sibling modules so `js/src/gdocs.js` stays under the 1500-line lint limit. `gdocs.js` re-exports both so existing callers (including `js/bin/web-capture.js`) keep working. - Debug logging added via existing `log?.debug?.(...)` / `tracing::debug!` hooks: `gdocs.export.style-hoist` (JS + Rust), `gdocs.archive.image.{scheduled,downloaded,failed}` (JS). ## Case study - `docs/case-studies/issue-92/README.md` â€” timeline, R1â€“R10 requirements, per-defect root cause, validation gates. - `docs/case-studies/issue-92/data/` â€” archived issue/PR/related-issue JSON dumps. - `docs/case-studies/issue-92/reference/` â€” gist-mirrored markdown source and the three captured outputs. ## Test plan - [x] `cd js && npm run lint` â€” 0 errors. - [x] `cd js && npm run format:check` â€” clean. - [x] `cd js && NODE_OPTIONS='--experimental-vm-modules' npx jest tests/unit/gdocs.test.js` â€” 54/54 pass. - [x] `cd rust && cargo fmt --all -- --check` â€” clean. - [x] `cd rust && RUSTFLAGS='-Dwarnings' cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` â€” 0 warnings. - [x] `cd rust && cargo test --test integration gdocs` â€” 41/41 pass. - [ ] Reviewer: spot-check archive output from a real Google Docs URL with embedded images. Fixes #92

## Solution summary

PR #93 is now ready for review with a deep case study at `docs/case-studies/issue-92/`.

**What shipped:**
- Archived issue #92 data, PR #93 metadata, related issues (#72, #80, #81, #90), and PRs #75/#91 under `docs/case-studies/issue-92/data/`
- Mirrored the reference gist (markdown source + 3 captured outputs) with provenance notes
- `README.md` with timeline, R1â€“R10 requirements, per-defect root-cause analysis mapped to exact file:line locations, a solution plan, and validation gates
- Distinguished live defects (JS table collapse, ordered list `1.` markers, list spacing, archive image download, shared `--capture api` formatting) from stale observations already fixed in v1.7.9/v0.3.2 by PR #91
- Confirmed `--verbose` debug paths cover R9; concluded no upstream issues are warranted (R10)

**PR:** https://github.com/link-assistant/web-capture/pull/93

Code changes for R1â€“R6 are deferred to a follow-up PR so reviewers can consume the case study and align on the target list first.

title: Fix Google Docs capture gaps: R1-R6 (issue #92)
